content.problemStatement: You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and an integer KKK. Determine if it is possible to reorder the elements of the sequence AAA into a sequence B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã and choose a real number mmm in such a way that for each contiguous subsequence of BBB with length KKK, the arithmetic average of the elements of this subsequence is mmm. If it is possible, find one such reordered subsequence BBB.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"YES"` if it is possible to reorder the elements of AAA in the required way, or `"NO"` otherwise.

If it is possible, print a second line containing NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã. If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one.
